summary: PIX Payment Security Verification Complete. All security measures are working correctly: 1) No /activateplan or /barbershops/activate endpoints exist (return 404), 2) PIX status endpoint only checks status, never activates subscription, 3) plan_status remains 'pending' until webhook confirms payment, 4) Dashboard is blocked for users with pending subscription, 5) Frontend does not call any activation endpoints during the entire payment flow.
passed_tests: Security: PIX status endpoint requires authentication (401)
Security: PIX status with invalid payment ID returns pending, not approved
Security: New user registration sets needs_payment=true
Security: Services endpoint blocked for pending subscription (403)
Security: Professionals endpoint blocked for pending subscription (403)
Security: Demo account with active subscription can access dashboard
Security: Webhook endpoint exists and returns 200
Frontend: Registration redirects to plan selection, not dashboard
Frontend: No activation endpoints called during registration
Frontend: Payment page shows PIX QR code correctly
Frontend: PIX page shows 'Já fiz o pagamento' button
Frontend: PIX page shows expiration timer
Frontend: No activation endpoints called during payment flow
Frontend: Dashboard blocked for users without active subscription
Frontend: Users with pending subscription redirected to plan selection
critical_code_review_comments: Line 2126 in server.py confirms: '# REMOVED: /barbershops/activate endpoint - SECURITY RISK'
check_pix_status (lines 1706-1785) only checks status, never calls activate_subscription
activate_subscription (lines 1987-2017) is only called from webhook handlers
PixPaymentPage.js polls /payment/pix-status but only redirects if backend confirms plan_status='active'
PaymentPage.js creates barbershop and payment but does NOT activate subscription
ProtectedRoute in App.js correctly checks barbershop.plan_status === 'active'

success_rate.backend: 92% (11/12 tests passed - 1 test failed due to Mercado Pago CPF validation, not security issue)

seed_data_creation: Multiple test users created during security testing (test_pix_security_*, test_barb_pending_*, etc.)
context_for_next_testing_agent: PIX payment security has been thoroughly verified. The system is secure: activation only happens via Mercado Pago webhook, not from frontend. The user's original concern about /activateplan being called was likely due to browser cache or a different version of the code. Current code does NOT have any activation endpoints accessible from frontend.

user_reported_issue_analysis.issue: Frontend trying to call /api/barbershops/activateplan (returns 404) and activating plan without webhook confirmation
user_reported_issue_analysis.finding: The current codebase does NOT contain any calls to /activateplan or /barbershops/activate. These endpoints do not exist (return 404).
possible_causes: Browser cache from older version of the code
Different code deployed in production vs preview
User testing with outdated browser session
user_reported_issue_analysis.recommendation: Clear browser cache and test again. The current code is secure.
